Its lens acts as both eye and ear with precise vision and imprecise hearing. This recorded sequence can then be played back as many times as desired, the sights and sounds communicated telepathically to the rod's holder. The rod's recording sequence can be triggered manually by a person holding it, or it can be programmed to begin recording automatically in response to a specified stimulus, such as nearby movement or a specific trigger word spoken in its vicinity.

markdown: **Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** three times per day;   **Effect** You become undetectable to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures for 10 minutes. Undead can't see, hear, or smell you, nor detect you with sensory capabilities such as [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) or [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40). A creature can attempt a single DC 18 Will saving throw to ignore this effect. If an undead has reason to believe that undetected opponents are present, it can still attempt to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) or Strike you. If you attempt to use a [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) spell to damage undead, use [Command Undead](/Feats.aspx?ID=277), touch or damage an undead creature, or attack any creature while warded in this manner, the pendant's effects immediately end. An undead creature who observes you in this manner or one who succeeds at the Will save is immune to the _corpseward pendant_ for 24 hours.
name: Interact

<title level="1" right="Action">[Prove Peace](/Actions.aspx?ID=625) <actions string="Two Actions" /></title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=45) pg. 84

**Requirements** You know the conflict that created a specific siege shard, and the siege shard is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24).

</column>

---

You touch the siege shard, establish a mental connection, and relay facts of the conflict's resolution. Attempt a check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about the conflict that created the shard, using the siege shard's Will DC. If you succeed, you prove the conflict's end, and the siege shard reverts to ordinary stone. On a critical failure, images of the past conflict overwhelm you, and you are [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) until the end of your next turn.
name: Prove Peace

markdown: **Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command;     **Effect** You attempt to make the ring cast an arcane [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377) spell. However, if the ring doesn't deem the wish to be sufficiently selfless, the wish isn't cast and the ring can't be activated for 24 hours. You are [drained 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), whether or not the ring refuses the wish. Once the wish is cast, the _Ring of Recalcitrant Wishes_ has no powers, though it is rumored that the wearer's death in an act of profound self-sacrifice restores the _wish_ to the ring.

markdown: **Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You belch out a foul-smelling cloud of blisteringly spicy gas that fills a single square adjacent to you. Creatures within the cloud are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and all creatures outside the cloud are concealed to creatures within it. The cloud remains for 1 minute but can be dispersed by a strong wind. The cloud deals 1d4 fire damage to creatures that enter the cloud on their turn, as well as to creatures that start their turn in the cloud (a creature takes this damage no more than once per round, even if it moves back and forth into the cloud multiple times during the round).
name: Interact

markdown: **Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> concentrate, manipulate;     **Effect** You place the statue on solid ground and speak the rat's secret name, causing the statuette to transform into a living [giant rat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=346). In creature form, the giant rat acts on your turn. It gets 2 actions and can't use reactions. You have to spend an action each turn to tell it what to do; otherwise, it tries to run away from danger or cowers where it is. If the rat is slain while in animal form, it reverts to its statue shape and can't be transformed again until 1 week has passed. If the figurine is destroyed while in its statue form, it's shattered and its magical properties are lost forever.
name: concentrate, manipulate

<title level="1" right="Action">[Change Shape](/Actions.aspx?ID=700) <actions string="Single Action" /></title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 79

</column>

---

You change into your humanoid or hybrid shape. Each shape has a specific, persistent appearance, and most beastkin remain in their hybrid shapes by default. In hybrid shape, you appear as a mix between your ancestry and your inherent animal. While in hybrid shape, you gain a jaws unarmed Strike resembling the features of your inherent animal (fangs for bats, beaks for eagles, mandibles for wasps, and so on). Your jaws deal 1d4 piercing damage, have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) traits, and are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) weapon group. In your humanoid shape, you retain the appearance of your original ancestry.
name: Change Shape

<title level="1" right="Action">[Change Shape](/Actions.aspx?ID=701) <actions string="Single Action" /></title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Kitsune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=341" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 121

</column>

---

You transform into a specific alternate form determined by your heritage. If your heritage doesn't list a form, your alternate form is a tailless form, which is a common Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) ancestry prevalent where you grew up (typically [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6)). This form is the same age and body type as your true form and has roughly analogous physical traits, such as hair color. Using Change Shape counts as creating a disguise for the [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) use of [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5). You lose any unarmed Strikes you gained from a kitsune heritage or ancestry feat in this form. You can remain in your alternate form indefinitely, and you can shift back to your true kitsune form by using this action again.
name: Change Shape

Slowing or stopping this transformation via other means requires complex rituals or divine intervention, subject to the GM's whim. After 7 days, the body completes its transformation and Tchekuth rises once more as a physical threat.

<title level="1" right="Action">[Change Shape](/Actions.aspx?ID=730) <actions string="Single Action" /></title>

<traits>
<trait label="Anadi" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=255" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 103

</column>

---

You change into your human or spider shape. Each shape has a specific, persistent appearance. In your human shape, you can't use [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks granted by your ancestry. You aren't [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) when climbing in your spider shape. However, in your spider shape you can't use weapons, shields, or other held items of any sort, and you are limited in what actions you can take that have the [manipulate](/Actions.aspx?ID=639) trait. The only manipulate actions you can take are to Cast a Spell with somatic components, weave silk or webbing, or simple Interact actions such as opening an unlocked door. Your spider legs can't perform actions that require fingers or significant manual dexterity, including any action that would require a check to accomplish. The GM might determine other manipulate actions are appropriate for your spider legs.
name: Change Shape

<title level="1" right="Action">[Spellstrike](/Actions.aspx?ID=755) <actions string="Two Actions" /></title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=389" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 37

**Frequency** until recharged (see below)

</column>

---

You channel a spell into a punch or sword thrust to deliver a combined attack. You [cast a spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) that takes 1 or 2 actions to cast and requires either a spell attack roll or a saving throw. You imbue its effects into an attack instead of executing the spell normally. Make a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with a weapon or [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) attack. This counts as two attacks for your [multiple attack penalty](/Rules.aspx?ID=2289), but you don’t apply the penalty until after you’ve completed the Spellstrike. The infusion of spell energy grants your Strike the [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) trait, making it [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644). If the spell required a spell attack roll, use your Strike’s results to determine the effects of both the Strike and the spell. If it required a save, the target of the Strike rolls its saving throw normally, though if your Strike was a critical failure, the target is unaffected. 

After you use Spellstrike, you can’t do so again until you recharge your Spellstrike as a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. You also recharge your Spellstrike when you cast a conflux spell that takes at least 1 action to cast; casting a focus spell of another type doesn’t recharge your Spellstrike. 

<title level="2" right="">Spellstrike Specifics</title> Though the base Spellstrike rules cover most spells, various modifications apply to more complicated spells when loaded into your fist, blade, or other attack. 

**One Target**: The spell targets only the target of your Strike, even if it normally allows more targets. This includes area spells, which only the target attempts a saving throw against. Any ongoing effects of the area, such as the [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) from [_scatter scree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2021), affect only the target’s space. Some feats let you affect more creatures. 

**Reach**: The coupled spell affects the target using the reach of the weapon or unarmed attack you make your Spellstrike with. For instance, [_gouging claw_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1546) would affect a creature beyond the reach of your hand if you used a weapon with [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), and [_frostbite_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539) would affect only a creature in your weapon’s reach, even though the spell’s range is longer. 

**Ancillary Effects**: Your spell still has any non-targeted effects that might affect creatures other than the target, as well as any ongoing effects starting from the moment you hit with the Strike. For example, _scatter scree_ would still create difficult terrain in the target’s space (as mentioned above) and [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713)’s circumstance penalty would last for its normal duration. The spell takes effect after the Strike deals damage; if the Strike has other special effects, the GM determines whether they happen before or after the spell. 

**Multiple Defenses**: Any additional rolls after the initial spell attack still happen normally, such as the Fortitude save attempted by the target of a [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=76) spell. Similarly, a spell that allows you to attack with it again on subsequent rounds would only combine a Strike with its initial attack roll, not with any later ones.

 **Invalid or Immune Target**: If the target you hit wouldn't be a valid target for the spell, the spell is still expended but doesn't affect the target. If the target is immune to your attack but not the spell, it can still be affected by the spell.

 **Variable Actions**: Some spells have different effects based on the number of actions you spend to cast them. You choose whether to use the effects of the 1- or 2-action version of the spell when you use Spellstrike. A spell has to take _exactly_ 1 or 2 actions; you can't use Spellstrike with a spell that takes a free action, reaction, or 3 or more actions.

 **Spellshape**: You typically can’t use [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513) with Spellstrike because spellshape actions require the next action you take to be Cast a Spell, and Spellstrike is a combined activity that doesn’t qualify.
name: Spellstrike

<traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 38

**Requirements** You used your most recent action this turn to Cast a Spell or make a [Spellstrike](/Actions.aspx?ID=755). You need to meet this requirement only to enter the stance, not to remain in it.

</column>

---

You divert a portion of the spell's magical power and keep it cycling through your body and weapon using specialized forms, breathing, or footwork. While you're in the stance, your melee Strikes deal 1 extra force damage. This damage increases to 2 if you have weapon specialization and 3 if you have greater weapon specialization. Any Strike that benefits from this damage gains the [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11) trait, making it [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103).

If your most recent spell before entering the stance was one that can deal damage, the damage from the stance is instead the same type that spell could deal (or one type of your choice if the spell could deal multiple types of damage).
name: Arcane Cascade

Your eidolon appears in an open space adjacent to you, and can then take a single action. If your eidolon was already manifested, you unmanifest it instead.

 The conduit that allows your eidolon to manifest is also a tether between you. Your eidolon must remain within 100 feet of you at all times and can't willingly go beyond that limit. If forced beyond this distance, or if you are reduced to 0 Hit Points, your eidolon's physical form dissolves: your eidolon unmanifests, and you need to use Manifest Eidolon to manifest it again.

**Special** This activity has the trait matching your eidolon's tradition ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), or [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)).

You and your eidolon act as one. Either you or your eidolon takes an action or activity using the same number of actions as Act Together, and the other takes a single action. The GM determines the DC based on the [hard DC for the ley line's level](/Rules.aspx?ID=552). **Critical Success** You gain the ley line's benefits until the end of your next turn.
**Success** You gain the ley line's benefits until the end of your turn.
**Failure** You take mental damage equal to 1d6 × the ley line's level. You can't Tap the Ley Line again for 1 hour.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and you're subject to the ley line's backlash effect. You can't Tap the Ley Line again for 24 hours.
name: Tap Ley Line

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 245 --- You spend a week trying to discover and learn a creature's name. The exact form of your effort varies depending on the skill you use, the resources you have available, and other circumstances. Decide if you are searching for the name of a specific individual or for names in general. If you're looking for the name of an individual, you must be able to clearly identify that individual; for example, “the general leading the invasion” is enough, but “the person who killed the duchess” isn't, if you don't know who killed the duchess. If you're searching for names more generally, name one creature type. The GM chooses a DC, typically [based on the level](/Rules.aspx?ID=554) of the creature in question. If you're seeking names more generally, the DC is typically based on the level of the creature whose name the GM decides to provide, usually a creature from the chosen type of your level or lower. The GM might modify the DC of the task based on the resources you have available, or on using an unusually appropriate or inappropriate skill, or on other circumstances. Attempt a check with a skill that could be used to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about the creature's type. After attempting to Learn a Name, you typically can't try to learn the name of the same individual again unless you gain access to a substantial new source of information, as determined by the GM. **Critical Success** You find one or more private names of the specific individual you chose, or the private name of a creature with the type you chose and a level equal to the task level. You also find hidden fragments of their true name and, at the GM's discretion, you might find a clue leading to an adventure where you can learn the rest of the true name.
**Success** As critical success, except you find only one private name and don't find hidden fragments of their true name.
**Critical Failure** If you were searching for the name of a specific individual, you find no new information and that individual becomes aware of your efforts. If you were searching for a general name of a specific type, you find a creature's name or names likely to get you in trouble, possibly the names of a different type of creature entirely.
name: Learn Name
